item.dc:creator: peruvianfightingmidgets

item.title: I'm NOT famous (but I hang with famous people) Part 1
item.description:
Going through pics and I thought I'd share a few of my favourites from the last few years ... I was signing comics at Golden Apple in 2023 and the owner asks if I want to meet someone in the back ... SAM JACKSON is picking up comics! I ask the owner if I'm allowed to have a picture with him. Sam turns to the owner, and (in the Jules from PULP FICTION voice) says, "Why are you asking THIS motherfucker?! I'M the one you're taking the picture with!" I was kind of stunned and could barely muster a smile LOL But it was great to chat with a legend. He even asked for a signed copy of ARENA MODE! Best. Day. Ever.
Co-created a comic with EVANESCENCE lead singer Amy Lee in 2023, and I met her backstage at her concert in Toronto. AMAZING to meet her and the band, who were so kind and welcoming. We signed some copies of the book, and took some photos.
If you're into cosplayers you probably know ERICA FETT, who I absolutely LOVE! I try to hang out with her at every con we both attend.
Tehani Farr is an INCREDIBLE artist. She's so talented and beautiful. Love her. If you're into fantasy art, definitely check out her work. Her HEAVY METAL cover was amazing!
One of the most legendary comic book creators of all time, Walt Simonson. We've never worked on a project together, but who knows what the future holds? Fun fact: on his THOR run, he created both Beta Ray Bill AND Frog Thor!
And one of the sweetest people I know (and cosplayer for my BARBARELLA comic series) Rachie, aka 'Faces by Rachie'. She's a megastar at comic cons -- I did a signing with her at NYCC and the fans were rabid!
Apologies if you hate selfies ... to make up for it, here is a SHELFIE. Jareth wishes you a wonderful weekend. xox Love and hugs, Blake xoxo
item.dc:creator: BlakeNorthcott

item.title: As someone with Deaf family, friends and former students, here are my thoughts
item.description: First of all, the reason emergency services have an ASL interpreter on screen is to get the emergency information to the Deaf/hard of hearing/signing community. There are millions of people in the US (not trying to speak about other counties right now) that use ASL as a primary means of communication. Why isn’t captioning enough? Thank you for asking. If you’ve ever tried to watch live captioning there’s often a lot of errors/delays/ missing information. All things that can lead to death in an emergency situation. There’s also the fact that many people whose primary language is ASL may not be able to read/write English. I’ve worked with kids/adults who immigrated from other counties and were able to sign fluently but their English wasn’t up to “emergency evacuation details”. So, in summary, it’s to help people stay alive. If it doesn’t help you, then it’s not about you, and you should mind your own business. Thanks
item.dc:creator: sureasyourebornyourenevergoingtoseenounicorn

item.title: Interesting Movie Details. :)
item.description:
Actors in black and white movies were often putting their lives in danger during driving scenes, as they weren't able to tell if the traffic light was red or green.
During the filming of Jurassic Park (1993), T-Rex was known to sweat profusely as it was his first major role in 55 million years.
In Game of Thrones Season 8, Daenerys’ last words are “we break the wheel together” which spelled backwards says “rehtegot leehw eht kaerb ew” which doesn’t make sense just like the rest of season 8.
Ellen Ripley in the movie #Alien (1979) was originally written to be a man. Director Ridley Scott changed his mind when he watched The Hunger Games (2012) and realized that women can also be action movie leads
In Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle (2017), Dwayne Johnson's avatar did not have the power to read the map. This is because paper beats Rock. #movies #funny #the_more_you_know
VIN Diesel’s full legal name, Vehicle Identification Number Diesel, was the sole determining factor in his original casting for the Fast & Furious franchise.
In the movie Don't Look Up, Leonardo DiCaprio is shown married to a woman who's in her mid 40s . This confirms that the movie is sci-fi because it's impossible for DiCaprio to be with a woman if shes over 25. #movies #funny
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: Turned My Table Into Antarctica! Recreating Shackleton And The Endurance Legendary Journey!
item.description: I tried to be historically accurate, and inspired by some true events, like the football scene. The crew actually played that game on the ice. I hope you like this series. :)) If you'd like to see more of my works on IG: @ benedek.lampert The story behind the pictures: The Endurance expedition of 1914, led by Sir Ernest Shackleton, aimed to achieve the first crossing of the Antarctic continent. Known as the Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition, its goal was to traverse Antarctica from the Weddell Sea to the Ross Sea. However, the mission failed when the ship became trapped in pack ice, and the crew embarked on a heroic struggle for survival. After the Endurance became trapped in ice in January 1915, the crew drifted with the pack ice for months, hoping that natural movement would free the ship. By late October, the pressure of the ice became critical, severely damaging the vessel, which eventually sank on November 21. Shackleton and his crew then camped on the ice, trying to survive until the ice melt allowed them to reach Elephant Island with lifeboats. On August 30, Shackleton rescued all of his men from Elephant Island, concluding one of the most dramatic survival stories in Antarctic history.
item.dc:creator: Lampi95
